papers.title: Molecular Characterization of Rht-1 Dwarfing Genes in Hexaploid Wheat
papers.authors: Pearce S, Saville R, Vaughan SP, Chandler PM, Wilhelm EP, Sparks CA, Al-Kaff N, Korolev A, Boulton MI, Phillips AL, Hedden P, Nicholson P, Thomas SG

papers.title: N-terminal truncated RHT-1 proteins generated by translational reinitiation cause semi-dwarfing of wheat Green Revolution alleles
papers.authors: Van De Velde K, Thomas SG, Heyse F, Kaspar R, Van Der Straeten D, Rohde A

papers.title: The Impact of the Wheat Rht-B1b Semi-Dwarfing Allele on Photosynthesis and Seed Development Under Field Conditions
papers.authors: Jobson EM, Johnston RE, Oiestad AJ, Martin JM, Giroux MJ

papers.title: Novel Rht-1 dwarfing genes: tools for wheat breeding and dissecting the function of DELLA proteins
Two open reading frames of Rht-B1b acting as brake and throttle contributed to wheat Green Revolution
papers.authors: Chen Y, Yang Z, Wang H, Xia C, Zhang L, Sun J, Kong X, Liu X

edges.mechanism: GA binds TaGID1 pocket inducing conformational change
edges.evidence_sentence: Binding of bioactive GAs within a pocket in the GID1 protein induces a conformational change that permits interaction between the DELLA and GID1 proteins

edges.mechanism: GA-bound GID1 binds DELLA N-terminal DELLA/TVHYNP motif, targets for degradation
edges.evidence_sentence: This leads to closing of the N-terminal lid of GID1, allowing DELLAs to bind through their conserved N-terminal DELLA/TVHYNP motifs

edges.mechanism: GA-bound GID1 targets DELLA for degradation, applies to all homeologs
edges.evidence_sentence: Yeast two-hybrid assays demonstrated strong interactions between each putative TaGID1 and the wheat DELLA proteins RHT-A1, RHT-B1, and RHT-D1 in the presence of GA3

edges.mechanism: SCF-SLY1 F-box ubiquitinates DELLA for proteasomal degradation
edges.evidence_sentence: GID1/DELLA binding enables recognition by the GID2/SLEEPY1 F-box component of an SCF Ub E3 ligase, leading to ubiquitination of DELLAs and their subsequent 26S proteasome-mediated degradation

edges.mechanism: DELLA protein represses GA-responsive growth including stem elongation
edges.evidence_sentence: DELLA proteins, which act to repress GA-responsive growth, and their mutant alleles Rht-B1b and Rht-D1b are thought to confer dwarfism by producing more active forms of these growth repressors

edges.mechanism: DELLA protein homeolog represses GA-responsive stem elongation
edges.evidence_sentence: Rht-B1 and Rht-D1 encode DELLA proteins, which act to repress GA-responsive growth

edges.mechanism: GA induces DELLA degradation via GID1-SCF-SLY1 pathway; bioactive GAs targeted rapid degradation
edges.evidence_sentence: Bioactive GAs relieve the DELLA growth repression by targeting their rapid degradation through a GID1-GA receptor-mediated signalling pathway

edges.mechanism: GA20ox catalyses biosynthetic steps converting GA12 and GA53 to bioactive GA precursors; expression strongest in elongating stem nodes
edges.evidence_sentence: expression of the biosynthetic genes is strongest in growing tissues, with TaGA20ox1 expression located most strongly at the nodes

edges.mechanism: GA3ox catalyses final biosynthesis step to bioactive GA; expressed in peduncle and elongating internode
edges.evidence_sentence: TaGA3ox2 is most highly expressed in the peduncle and lower part of the P-1 internode

edges.mechanism: Rht-B1b stop-codon allele produces N-terminally truncated protein insensitive to GA; strong repressor
edges.evidence_sentence: Expression of these N-terminal truncated proteins in transgenic lines demonstrated their ability to cause strong dwarfism

edges.mechanism: Rht-B1b NIL reduces flag leaf photosynthetic rate 18% and chlorophyll A 23%
edges.evidence_sentence: at anthesis, Rht-B1b reduces flag leaf photosynthetic rate per unit area by 18% and chlorophyll A content by 23%

edges.mechanism: Rht-B1b NIL reduces plant height 23% in field (cv Mercia)
5'-ORF uORF brake/3'-ORF throttle dual-ORF translational control of Rht-B1b contributes to balanced semi-dwarfism
edges.evidence_sentence: Two ORFs of Rht-B1b act as a brake-throttle system to appropriately reduce plant height, contributing to the Green Revolution in wheat

perturbations.evidence_sentence: the Rht-B1b and Rht-D1b alleles produce only about a 20% reduction in height
perturbations.notes: Semi-dwarfing N-terminal stop-codon allele; 20% height reduction vs Rht-B1a WT

perturbations.evidence_sentence: the Rht-B1b and Rht-D1b alleles produce only about a 20% reduction in height
Rht-B1c and Rht-D1c are about half the size of the appropriate tall controls
perturbations.notes: Severe dwarf allele; 30-aa insertion in DELLA domain; ~50% height reduction

perturbations.evidence_sentence: Rht-B1c and Rht-D1c are about half the size of the appropriate tall controls
perturbations.notes: Extreme dwarf; 4-fold gene amplification of Rht-D1b

perturbations.evidence_sentence: Plants carrying the Rht-B1d mutation are less severely dwarfed than those carrying Rht-B1b alone
it is likely that the Rht-B1e mutation confers its GA-insensitive semidwarf phenotype in a manner analogous to Rht-B1b
wild-type alleles designated as Rht-A1a, Rht-B1a, and Rht-D1a
NILs varying for Rht-B1b, Rht-D1b, Rht-B1c, Rht-B1b+Rht-D1b
